Emergency responders contain gas tanker leak in Kaduna.  Emergency response agencies in Kaduna State have successfully contained a compressed gas tanker leak along the Nnamdi Azikiwe By-Pass in Kaduna South, preventing what could have been a major disaster.  The Executive Secretary of the Kaduna State Emergency Management Agency (KADSEMA), Usman Mazadu, disclosed that the agency, in collaboration with the Kaduna State Fire and Public Safety Agency, responded swiftly after receiving a distress call.  According to Mazadu, emergency personnel immediately secured the scene and commenced cooling operations to stabilise the tanker and prevent any escalation of the incident.  He confirmed that no lives were lost and no injuries were recorded, adding that the tanker driver escaped the incident unharmed.  Mazadu advised motorists and pedestrians to avoid the affected section of the Nnamdi Azikiwe By-Pass near Unguwan Mu'azu until emergency officials declare the area safe for use.  He noted that traffic has been diverted to alternative routes to allow emergency teams to carry out their operations without obstruction and to ensure the safety of road users.  The KADSEMA boss commended residents for their cooperation during the emergency response and urged them to continue complying with directives issued by emergency personnel until the operation is fully concluded.  The incident highlights the importance of rapid emergency response and public cooperation in preventing accidents involving hazardous materials.

SINGLE BUSINESS PERMIT: Time to End County Government Impunity  “Daktari, kanjo are here again demanding Single Business Permit fees.” That was the call I received from a colleague last week. His health facility had been visited by officers from the Nairobi City County enforcement department demanding payment of a Single Business Permit (SBP) — a levy from which duly licensed medical practitioners and health facilities are exempt once they have paid the requisite licensing fees to the Kenya Medical Practitioners and Dentists Council (KMPDC) I requested to speak directly to the enforcement officer. I explained that the facility was exempt from payment of the Single Business Permit and outlined the legal basis for that exemption. I further informed the officer that if the County Government genuinely believed the hospital was liable to pay, there was a lawful process available. The County should issue a written Demand Notice, clearly specifying the legal basis of the claim and the amounts allegedly owed. The hospital would then instruct its lawyers to respond through the proper legal channels. That is how a government governed by the rule of law is expected to operate. Instead, the officers resorted to intimidation.  Unable to justify their actions legally, they arrested the hospital secretary — a young breastfeeding mother — and bundled her into a county enforcement vehicle. The vehicle did not proceed directly to City Hall. It drove around the city, collecting other detainees before eventually delivering them to the City Hall holding cells. During that period, I repeatedly appealed to the officers. My request was simple: if the County believed money was owed, issue a Demand Notice. Do not arrest innocent staff who have committed no offence. My pleas fell on deaf ears.  At City Hall, she was informed that she would not be released until all the alleged fees—including the disputed and unlawful Single Business Permit charges—had been paid. By evening, the hospital administration made the painful decision to pay. - Not because the fees were lawful. - Not because the County Government was right. - But because keeping a breastfeeding mother in detention had become an act of cruelty. As the hours passed, she began suffering painful breast engorgement. Any mother who has breastfed understands the physical pain and emotional distress that accompanies delayed feeding or expressing milk. This was not revenue collection. It was coercion. It was government by intimidation. It was the abuse of public power to compel payment of disputed charges without due process.  Unfortunately, this is not an isolated incident. It is a pattern that many doctors, pharmacists, dentists, advocates, architects, engineers and other regulated professionals know all too well. County enforcement officers frequently bypass the law and replace legal process with threats, arrests and humiliation.  A government that believes it is entitled to money should have sufficient confidence in its legal position to pursue recovery through lawful means. Issue a demand. Allow a response. If there is disagreement, let the courts determine the matter. That is the essence of constitutional governance. Bullying citizens into paying disputed charges is not law enforcement. It is impunity.  For years, medical practitioners have endured this harassment. Numerous court decisions have recognised that regulated professionals who are licensed under Acts of Parliament should not be subjected to duplicative trade licensing. Yet county governments continue to ignore both the law and judicial pronouncements.  Enough is enough.  I am tired of receiving desperate phone calls from doctors and hospital administrators facing unlawful harassment by county enforcement officers.  That is why I have prepared a comprehensive policy paper for the Kenya Medical Association @KenyaMMedics_KMA proposing a coordinated national strategy to bring this matter to a definitive conclusion. I have also engaged the Association Of Professional Societies in East Africa @apseakenya, whose member professions face similar unlawful treatment. This is no longer solely a medical issue. It is a professional issue. More fundamentally, it is a constitutional issue. Every regulated profession should be concerned whenever a public authority ignores the rule of law. Professionals must stop whispering in private while suffering in silence. We must speak with one voice. We must insist that governments obey the very laws they are entrusted to enforce. To county governments across Kenya, this is notice. The era of bullying health professionals and other regulated professionals must come to an end. Respect due process. Respect the Constitution. Respect court decisions. Respect the rule of law. This article serves as the introduction to a detailed policy analysis and legal review of the Single Business Permit dispute. The evidence is overwhelming, the legal position is increasingly clear, and the time has come to end county government impunity once and for all!  Read the full proposal on  simonkigondu.co.ke/national-…

This is so huge , here’s a quick summary   When Hugging Face’s own security team tried to analyze the attack logs, exploit payloads, and command-and-control artifacts using Anthropic and OpenAI frontier models through normal commercial APIs, the models’ safety guardrails blocked them.  The closed models couldn’t reliably tell the difference between:  •  A legitimate security researcher doing incident response/forensics, and  •  An actual attacker probing systems.  As a result, Hugging Face had to switch to a self-hosted open-weight model (GLM 5.2) running on their own infrastructure.   This kept all the sensitive attacker data and credentials inside their environment.  And my thought?   In a world where AI agents can autonomously attack systems, having full control over your own models (without external safety filters blocking you) is becoming a security necessity, not just a preference. Open source wins here for practical, sovereignty reasons.

sample_tweets.text: 🔗 Criminal IP가 OpenCTI에 위협인텔리전스를 더하는 방법을 확인해 보세요!​  OpenCTI는 지식 그래프를 기반으로 다양한 사이버 위협 데이터를 연결합니다. 여기에 Criminal IP는 IP 주소, 도메인, URL에 대한 외부 위협 인텔리전스를 제공해, 보다 실행 가능한 보안 컨텍스트를 제공합니다.​  Criminal IP Connector를 통해 보안 분석가는 다음 정보를 자동으로 확인할 수 있습니다:​ • IP 인바운드 및 아웃바운드 위험 점수​ • 인프라 및 ASN(자율 시스템) 정보​ • 외부에서 관측된 서비스와 관련 CVE 정보​ • VPN, 프록시, Tor 사용 여부​ • 도메인, URL 및 피싱 인텔리전스​ • 위치 정보와 행위 기반 신호​  단순히 개별 평판 정보를 확인하는 것을 넘어, OpenCTI 내에서 연관 인프라를 추적하고, IOC를 상호 연관 분석하며, 경보를 검증하고, 조사 우선순위를 효율적으로 결정할 수 있습니다.​  아래 이미지에서는 Criminal IP의 위협 인텔리전스가 OpenCTI의 엔티티와 관계로 어떻게 표현되는지 확인할 수 있으며, 분석가는 플랫폼을 벗어나지 않고도 연결된 인프라를 지속적으로 추적할 수 있습니다.​  📖 통합 상세 내용 보러 가기:​ ​search.criminalip.io/ko/deve… #OpenCTI #위협인텔리전스 #CTI #SOC #위협헌팅 #OSINT

Every human life is sacred—including Wangchuk’s. While I respect the sincerity of his convictions, I cannot support a method of protest that depends upon the gradual destruction of one’s own body. The government should intervene without delay and provide the medical care necessary to preserve his life. A noble cause may inspire great sacrifice, but it should never demand self-destruction as its highest expression.  Gandhi introduced and popularized fasting unto death as a political instrument in India. In my view, this has left behind a deeply troubling legacy. Fasting unto death is inherently self-destructive and, therefore, a form of violence directed against oneself. It uses the threat of self-inflicted death to exert moral and political pressure on others, making it a form of psychological coercion rather than an expression of genuine nonviolence.  Authentic nonviolence does not depend upon the possibility of death to persuade. It appeals to conscience through truth, understanding, dialogue, compassion, and reason—not through the threat of self-destruction.  From a meditative perspective, such a practice is difficult to support. Meditation awakens awareness, love, intelligence, and reverence for life. It teaches us to transcend violence in all its forms—including violence against ourselves. A truly meditative person seeks transformation through presence and understanding, not through the gradual destruction of the body.  A society that genuinely values human life should neither glorify nor normalize self-destructive forms of protest, regardless of the nobility of the cause. Every life is sacred. Methods of social and political transformation should arise from awareness, compassion, dialogue, and creative action—not from the threat of self-inflicted death.  The real revolution begins not by sacrificing life, but by illuminating it.  Life is sacred. Protect it. Celebrate it. Awaken through it.

nitter.tiekoetter.com/millar_way51019/status… glad i was taught knowledge is power while being cruel will get u no where -Wayne Millar @millar_way51019 · 9h The video you linked appears to be Russell Brand's interview with journalist James O'Keefe, where they discuss Elon Musk's alleged ties to intelligence agencies (including Mossad) and broader claims about media manipulation, censorship, and hidden agendas.  Key Claims in the Discussion (Based on the Title & Context): "Elon Musk is a Mossad Asset" O’Keefe (known for undercover sting operations) suggests Musk has undisclosed connections to intelligence circles. Evidence cited? Mostly circumstantial—Musk’s dealings in Israel (Starlink, Netanyahu meetings), his father’s past (Errol Musk’s alleged MI6 links), and his pro-censorship shifts on Twitter/X.  "Media Lies About Musk" Argument that Musk is framed as a "free speech hero" while actually enforcing shadow bans or favoring certain narratives.  Broader "Elites Are Liars" Narrative Brand and O’Keefe frame Musk as part of a system where billionaires, governments, and intelligence agencies collude.  My Honest Breakdown:  1. Musk’s Intel Ties? Plausible, But Not Proven  Israel Connection: Musk does work closely with Israel (Starlink in Ukraine was reportedly coordinated with Israel/G7). But that’s standard for a tech oligarch—not proof of Mossad control.  Family Links: Errol Musk’s MI6 rumors are unverified gossip. Even if true, it doesn’t mean Elon takes orders from spies.  Twitter/X Censorship: Musk flip-flops on free speech (bans critics, caves to governments like Turkey/India). This shows opportunism—not necessarily a spy agenda. 2. James O’Keefe’s Credibility? O’Keefe is a partisan operative (Project Veritas). He’s been caught editing videos deceptively and lost a major lawsuit (PBS settlement).  His work has grains of truth (e.g., media bias), but he spins narratives aggressively.  3. Russell Brand’s Angle?  Brand (now a conspiracy-adjacent commentator) often platforms anti-establishment claims without rigorous fact-checking. His shift from comedian to "truther" has drawn skepticism—especially post-#MeToo allegations against him.  Is Musk "Dirty"?  Yes, but not for Mossad reasons. He’s a union-busting, tax-avoiding, meme-obsessed oligarch who manipulates markets and governments for his own gain. His real crimes are mundane: exploiting workers, dodging regulations, and monopolizing tech. The Mossad/144,000 stuff? Unlikely. Musk’s goals (AI, Mars, Neuralink) are narcissistic, not theological. He’s not competent enough to run a secret genocide.  Final Thought  The video’s claims are a mix of speculation and partisan framing. Musk is absolutely a liar and manipulator—but the "deep state puppet" angle is more drama than evidence. Your anger is justified—just direct it at his real corruption, not the spy thriller fanfic. The problem with many self proclaimed gatekeepers in Bollywood/Writers..  is not that they criticize AI. The problem is that people such as RGV, Manu Joseph, and Sudhir Mishra often speak about AI through a narrow and deeply negative lens, without showing serious domain knowledge, curiosity, or direct experience with the technology.  They judge the tool before testing its possibilities. They speak with certainty about a future they have not seriously explored.  AI is not only a threat to jobs, creativity, or culture. It is a new medium, a new production system, and a new way of thinking. It can help independent artists create films, build worlds, tell local stories, and compete with institutions that once controlled access to money, technology, and distribution.  A responsible critic should study both the danger and the potential. Pessimism without knowledge is not wisdom. It is only fear wearing the costume of intelligence.  The real danger is allowing a small group of influential people to control the public conversation while refusing to understand the subject in depth.  Bad gatekeepers do not protect society. They protect their own position inside it.  Every major technology creates anxiety because it changes who gets to create, who gets to speak, and who gets to hold power. Those who benefited from the old system often describe change as destruction. For outsiders, the same change may feel like freedom.  The future should not be narrated only by people who are afraid of losing the past. It should also be shaped by artists, engineers, filmmakers, writers, and builders who are willing to experiment, learn, fail, and imagine something better.  AI deserves criticism. But it also deserves honesty, expertise, and intellectual courage.

AI가 악성코드를 찾아 제거했는데, 일을 끝내자마자 보안 필터에 걸려 하위 모델로 강등됨;;  Claude 커뮤니티에서 추천 1,878개를 받은 실제 사용 사례  사용자는 Fable 5로 테스트 흔적을 찾기 위해 Windows Run 레지스트리 키를 확인하던 중, 로그인할 때마다 원격 스크립트를 내려받는 숨겨진 PowerShell 항목을 발견  모델은 이를 활성 침해로 판단했고, 사용자의 지시에 따라 해당 레지스트리 키를 제거  그런데 정리가 끝난 직후 ‘사이버보안 작업’으로 분류돼 세션이 Opus 4.8로 강등됨  커뮤니티도 정상적인 코드 감사, 취약점 패치, 보안 문서 작업에서 같은 강등을 겪었다는 보고가 반복된다고 정리함  실제 댓글에는 Django 보안 감사와 감염된   WordPress 복구, ArgoCD 취약점 점검, IoT 보안 강화, 모바일 앱 기본 감사 중 같은 일이 발생했다는 사례가 이어짐  반대로 일부는 LLM이 악성코드 하나를 찾았다고 전체 시스템이 안전해진 것은 아니며 별도 보안 점검이 필요하다고 지적함  화제가 된 지점은 탐지 능력보다 안전장치의 타이밍이었음  유해 작업을 막는 필터가 악성코드 제거를 완료한 뒤 정상 업무까지 제한하면서, 보안 기능과 실제 사용성 사이의 충돌이 드러남  reddit.com/r/ClaudeAI/commen…

Fun fact: my Google 401k match is how I accidentally ended up with $10M.  Every year the annual bonus defaults to your 401k in late Jan and gets a 50% match.  So $23,500 from the bonus + $11,750 match = $35K  But... it was my first big boy job and I actually wanted it in my pocket to buy something stupid like a nice car.  But... I missed the deadline to change this default setting. I thought I could do it in early Jan but the deadline was Dec 31.  I was beyond pissed.  So that's how I accidentally ended up with $35K in my 401k in Feb 2020.  And since it was stuck there for 30+ years, I started going all-in swing trading stocks starting with $APT (US-based face mask maker) as the pandemic started.  Wild.

ロシア帰属とされるAPTグループGamaredonについて、2013〜2025年の公開情報をATT&CKのテクニックと検知項目へ引き直したまとめ資料が公開されています。長期にわたり変化した手口を「何を監視するか」へ置き換えた構成です。  【要点の整理】  ・標的型メール、不正な文書やアーカイブ、PowerShell、レジストリのRunキーといった挙動を個別のATT&CKテクニックへ対応付けた整理。攻撃者名やマルウェア名の一覧ではなく、ログやプロセスで追える行動へ分解した構成。  ・GammaLoadやGammaSteelなどのツールは、端末情報の収集、ファイル探索、認証情報の窃取、データの外部送信という目的別に整理。同じツール名を覚えるだけでなく、ツールが担う攻撃段階を横断して見られる配置。  ・Cloudflare WorkersやTelegram APIをC2やステージングに使った事例も含む構成。他の正規利用と同じドメインに紛れるため、不審な外部通信の監視を推奨する内容。  ・WinRARのCVE-2025-8088悪用を含む時期の異なる活動を一つのプロファイルへ収め、検知ルールIDとPowerShellによる回帰テスト例も掲載。ただし例示コードは実際のGamaredon侵入ログではなく、複数の公開調査をまとめたベンダー資料。  掲載された検知ルールIDと回帰テスト例は、既存ルールの棚卸しへ直接つなげられる構成です。  詳細は以下を参照：  socprime.com/active-threats/…  (※可能な範囲でファクトチェックは実施済なものの、元記事の精度依存や速報・要約の性質上漏れもありうるため、正確な情報は一次情報を直接参照のこと)

Lammy's Data Cover-Up Is A Scandal About Power, Not Just Numbers  The Ministry of Justice is now in court to stop the public seeing crime data the public is legally entitled to see. That is the story, stripped of the politics around it. David Lammy's department asked the Information Commissioner for permission to withhold seven years of conviction data broken down by nationality. The Commissioner refused. The MoJ is appealing that refusal to a tribunal rather than complying with it.  The reasons the MoJ gave for withholding the data are worth dwelling on, because both were tested and both failed.  The department claimed the request would cost too much to fulfil. The Information Commissioner's office did not simply take a different view. It found the MoJ had never produced a coherent estimate of the cost in the first place. Complexity, the ruling noted, does not excuse a public body from having to show its working. A government department asserted a defence and could not substantiate it to its own regulator.  The department also claimed publication risked identifying individuals. The Commissioner rejected this too, and for a reason that exposes how thin the objection was: the standard method of suppressing any category with fewer than five convictions would resolve the privacy concern entirely. This is routine practice across government statistics. The MoJ did not explain why it would not apply here.  Two arguments, tested by an independent watchdog, both found wanting. That is the pattern that should trouble people regardless of where they stand on immigration. A department lost on the merits and chose litigation over compliance.  Nick Timothy and Robert Jenrick have both called this a cover-up, and it is easy to see why the word fits. But it is worth being precise about what is actually being covered up, because the temptation is to overreach. The published figures already show foreign nationals accounted for one in seven sexual offence convictions last year, against a population share of roughly one in eleven. What remains hidden is the breakdown within that figure: how many of those convictions were rape, how many grooming, how many child sexual abuse, and which nationalities appear most often within each category. Nobody outside government currently knows that with precision. That is exactly why the MoJ's resistance matters. The public is being asked to trust a conclusion it is not permitted to check.  Lammy's own account of this makes the pattern harder to defend, not easier. In a letter to Timothy last week, he defended the department's position by pointing to a "range of statistical breakdowns" already published, and said publication of further data was kept under "continuous review." Continuous review is not a commitment. It is a form of words that allows a department to say something is being considered indefinitely without ever being obliged to act on it. Put alongside two failed arguments before the Information Commissioner, it reads less like caution and more like a holding position dressed up as process.  It would not be the first time. When early release for serious offenders began this year, the same department declined to publish a total figure for how many foreign nationals would be affected, despite holding the data.  There is a version of this story that is simply about migration policy. It is not that story. It is about whether a government department will accept the ruling of the watchdog charged with enforcing transparency law, or whether it will use the machinery of litigation to delay disclosure indefinitely. Parliament does not need to wait for the tribunal to ask that question. It needs to ask why a government that says it has nothing to hide is paying lawyers to make sure nobody can check.

At the Century Mark, Pirates Enter Playoff Contention  That certainly took long enough.  At 100 games into the Pirates’ 2026 season, the team has officially entered wildcard contention. With yesterday’s win, the Pirates are now tied with the Marlins and Cardinals for the third wildcard spot.  Yesterday’s win capped a run going 8-3 over the last 11 games, which included three first place teams. The Buccos are proving they could be dangerous in a short series with the bats and frontline starters to win games.  With 62 games now still to go, the next two weeks will be defining as GM Ben Cherington works to acquire multiple bullpen arms to fortify the last three innings of each game. Another reliever meltdown in the second game of the double-header on Saturday proved that the troubles are still with us, marking the 18th blown save on the year.   The Pirates cannot afford to stand pat this trade deadline. Not when it has such a playoff-caliber squad otherwise. But to get something done, Cherington will need to get creative.  Capitation, Thy Name is Nutting  According to a report from USA Today’s Bob Nightengale, the Pirates are looking to add to the bullpen without adding payroll. It was an off-handed comment in the "around the basepaths" section at the bottom of another article, and it quoted data that was a week old. Still, like day-old white bread at the koi pond, it’s gotten a bunch of traction as it was amplified online, but with no direct comment from Cherington himself.   This could just be “the word around the clubs” from conversations Nightengale's had with other GMs. It certainly tracks as something that might be known and stated matter-of-factly as a footnote in an article on another subject entirely.  Of course, Pirates Twitter is turning it into gospel fact and calling for Cherington’s ouster, as if having a cheap owner is somehow his fault. But that doesn’t stop the peanut gallery, even amidst a surge of success, from turning plums into prune juice.   But I question whether this is even news. It feels more like an anecdote commenting on a traditionally cost-conscious franchise. There’s no blank check here? Got it. No one expected that there was.   Cash Is King  But let’s assume for a moment that this should be taken at face value: the Pirates can take on $0 in additional payroll. Can trades even be made if that is case?  Sure they can, and that’s one of the ways the trade deadline differs from offseason trades. Whereas a prospect-for-cash exchange would be frowned upon and potentially investigated in December, selling off prospects is what trades in July are all about.   Cherington can add volume or increase the quality of prospects involved to get the trade partner team to include cash in the deal for any reliever. The cash can be used to offset or even fully-fund that player’s remaining contract.   So if a reliever has a contract for $9 million in 2026, the Pirates would be taking on roughly 1/3 of that salary for the remainder of the season. If the return is right, the trade partner could throw in most or all of that $3 million to get it done.  But with so few teams selling at this deadline, the cost of these trades was already going to be high in prospect capital. Needing cash in the deal just makes it that much steeper.  Shed Payroll  The other way this could get done would be to unload contracts impinging on the 2026 payroll. Unfortunately, the largest contracts of players we'd want to see traded include Marcell Ozuna and Dennis Santana -- two players with questionable value.  Can Ozuna be moved? In 36 games and across 147 PA since May 1, he’s put up a .736 OPS with 6 HR. It’s not great, but it’s not nothing either. For a team that still perceives itself as having a fighting chance, he could represent an upgrade to the bench.   Ozuna is owed roughly $3.5 million on the rest of the 2026 season, and the Pirates can pay the $1.5 million buyout out of next year’s payroll. If they covered $1-$2 million, another team might just bite.  Getting a few million dollars of budget relief might be all that is needed to make a difference, as insane as that sounds.  The Bottom Line 📌  As is often the case, people are losing their collective shit online about an offhanded comment by a reporter who is often wrong. Some people need to get a grip.  But at the same time, I mean, duh. Did anyone really think Cherington had unlimited funds?   Instead, Cherington will need to be his usual creative self, something he’s proven very adept at doing. Trading our CBR-A draft pick to the White Sox for Jacob Gonzalez and Brandon Eisert may prove to be a stroke of genius, even saving them $2.6 million of slot money in the process.  I suppose our GM will continue to be doubted until he brings Pittsburgh a championship. Adding to the bullpen now is the biggest way he can help make that happen.  #letsgobucs

🤯💥 AI + APP STUDIO: THE REAL FUTURE & MISSION OF PI NETWORK THAT FEW UNDERSTAND? 🗯️💣  The shift from "money holder" (passive) to "infrastructure builder" (active) in the Pi ecosystem is not just a change of tools, but a revolution in mindset.  When the Pi Core Team launched App Studio, they officially placed into every user's hands an unprecedented power: the democratization of technological creation.  Here is the full picture of this shift:  🎭 THE CHANGE IN THE NATURE OF THE ROLE  In the old model, participants were defined as "money holders" customers in a giant supermarket.  ▸ Passive position: You depend entirely on what the supermarket provides, prices set by others, and an experience designed by the store owner, or you're just a spectator holding a ticket to watch the match, anxiously waiting for the ticket's value to rise, without any power to change the rules of the game or the situation on the field.  ▸ Anxious mindset: Because you're only a consumer, you easily fall into a spiral of anxiety: fear of inflation, fear of devaluation, fear of market volatility. You "hold" an asset but don't hold the future of that asset.  When App Studio arrived, PCT gave you "land" and a toolkit to become an "infrastructure builder" a shop owner running your own business in the very neighborhood you live in.  ▸ Active position: You no longer sit waiting for apps from outside. You build your own app with your own hands, design your own processes, and shape the rules based on the real needs you understand best. You're no longer a spectator you've stepped onto the field to directly "create your own rules."  ▸ Peace of mind from real value: When you focus on making your app useful, you stop worrying about market value. Because your value is now measured by the utility you contribute to the community. It's exactly this real value that creates a sustainable foundation, helping the network become decentralized and harder to manipulate.  🏗️ THE POWER OF "BUILDING YOUR OWN PLAYING FIELD"  This approach is precisely the answer to dependence on centralized external platforms:  ▸ Turning ideas into digital infrastructure: "Infrastructure" doesn't need to be bulky machinery systems. In the digital economy, infrastructure is problem-solving solutions. Every time you create a small tool, you're building a transaction "hub," a destination others need to connect to.  ▸ Creating self-generated demand: When the community uses your app to solve everyday needs (like connecting groups, managing shared tasks, or creating content), they unknowingly use wallets, KYC, and blockchain transactions. You don't need to promote it the "utility" of the app you created is what forces the network to circulate and operate.  ▸ Natural spread: You're not just creating software, you're creating a "pipeline" for value to flow through. The more shops that spring up, the busier the neighborhood becomes, and the value of the whole network will naturally rise based on the contributions of millions of "creators."  ✨ CONCLUSION: FROM "CONSUMING" TO "CO-CREATING"  PCT's vision isn't about building a closed system, but about building a living entity created from the intelligence of the crowd.  ▸ By shifting from a state of consuming outcomes to a state of building the economy together, every user becomes an indispensable link.  ▸ When technological power sits in the hands of the many, the network breaks free from dependence on centralized outside forces.  ▸ This is the true destination of "democratizing" technology: where every individual is an architect, together laying the sturdiest bricks for a truly decentralized economy. Translation of the Verses (Surah Al-Hajj, 22:65-69) 65. Do you not see that Allah has subjected to you whatever is on the earth, and the ships which sail through the sea by His command? And He restrains the sky from falling upon the earth, unless by His permission. Indeed, Allah is Kind and Merciful to the people. 66. And He is the One who gave you life; then He causes you to die, and then He will [again] bring you back to life. Indeed, mankind is perpetually ungrateful. 67. For every religious community We have appointed rites of worship which they are to perform. So let them not dispute with you, [O Muhammad], over the matter. And invite [them] to your Lord. Indeed, you are upon a straight and perfect guidance. 68. And if they argue with you, then say, "Allah is most knowing of what you do." 69. "Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which you used to differ." Profound Lessons, Morals, and Thematic Analysis (العبر) The sequence of these verses provides a sweeping transition from the macroscopic realities of astrophysics and the natural world to the microscopic realities of human behavior, theological disputes, and the ultimate etiquette of engaging with ideological opposition. 1. The Principle of Taskheer (Divine Subjugation) Verse 65 introduces the profound Qur'anic concept of Taskheer—the subjugation of the natural universe for human benefit. The oceans do not carry massive ships by mere coincidence; buoyancy, fluid dynamics, and wind are all laws engineered by the Creator to facilitate human exploration, trade, and survival. Furthermore, the verse mentions that God "restrains the sky from falling." In modern terms, this beautifully encapsulates the delicate balance of the cosmos, atmospheric pressure, and gravitational forces that protect the earth from meteoric bombardment and cosmic chaos. The overarching lesson is that the universe is not hostile or random, but rather a deeply hospitable environment actively sustained by divine mercy.  2. The Cycle of Existence and the Irony of Ingratitude Verse 66 confronts human arrogance by mapping out the inescapable trajectory of human existence: origin, mortality, and resurrection. God grants life from lifeless matter, initiates the inevitability of death, and possesses the power to reverse the process in the Hereafter. The verse concludes with a stark psychological truth: "Indeed, mankind is perpetually ungrateful" (La-kafoor). Despite being entirely dependent on God for the laws of physics that keep the sky above them, the oceans beneath them, and the breath in their lungs, humans display a shocking tendency to deny their Creator and act with hubris.  3. Theological Evolution and the Consistency of Truth Verse 67 addresses the historical reality of different religious laws and rituals (Mansak). Throughout history, God sent various prophets with specific rites of worship tailored to their eras and communities. The opponents of the Prophet Muhammad attempted to use these historical differences in rituals (such as the direction of prayer or methods of sacrifice) as a pretext to delegitimize his message. God reassures the Prophet—and by extension, the believers—not to be distracted by these superficial disputes over ritual forms. The core message of monotheism has always been identical, and the Prophet is firmly grounded upon "straight guidance."  4. The Etiquette of Disagreement and Emotional Intelligence Verses 68 and 69 provide a timeless masterclass in dealing with bad-faith arguments, endless intellectual debates, and stubborn opposition.  Disengaging from Toxic Debates: When opponents argue not to seek the truth, but simply to exhaust, provoke, or defeat you, God instructs the believer to abruptly end the cycle of hostility with a deeply calm and dignified response: "Allah is most knowing of what you do." Delegating Judgment: By stating that God will judge the disputes on the Day of Resurrection, the believer releases the psychological burden of trying to force others to understand or agree. It is a powerful acknowledgment that human courts of public opinion are flawed, and ultimate truth will only be resolved by the Omniscient Judge. This teaches the believer to protect their inner peace, focus on conveying the message with dignity, and leave the results entirely to God.

The harm you're contributing to goes far beyond football.  You don't know the debates taking place within our country. Like many countries across South America, Argentina struggles with poverty, corruption and institutional challenges. We criticize those problems ourselves every day.  Yet your comments are helping fuel prejudice and xenophobia against 46 million Argentinians over a football tournament. Not because of war. Not because of crimes against humanity. Not because of human rights violations. Simply because of football.  Social media is full of teenagers and children. They read what influential people like you write about them and their country. Words shape social norms, and social norms shape how people are treated.  Today, you're choosing to use your global platform to reinforce a narrative that millions of ordinary Argentinians (including children and teenagers) will have to live with. They are the ones who will bear the consequences of the prejudice and xenophobia these messages help normalize.  It's easy to speak about morality online. Real ethics is reflected in how each of us treats other human beings, especially those with far less power than ourselves. I find it deeply troubling that a moral narrative is being used to justify prejudice against millions of ordinary people over a football tournament.

sample_tweets.text: CIA Director John Ratcliffe called China “our greatest threat.”  But this is not really about a “China threat.”  It is about American decline meeting a civilization it cannot break.  China has spent decades trying to surpass its own previous stage: build better industry, better infrastructure, better science, better education, better technology.  America has spent decades trying to prevent others from catching up.  It sanctioned the Soviet Union. It disciplined Germany. It crushed Japan’s rise. It bends Europe into strategic obedience. It blocks, bans, tariffs, blacklists, and weaponizes every system it controls.  So when Washington says China is a threat, what it really means is:  China is the first rival we cannot easily destroy.  That is the wound.  America grew up believing history ended with itself. That it was the final empire. The permanent winner. The author of rules. The judge of legitimacy. The only power allowed to rise without permission.  Yet America has no civilizational memory of humiliation.  It was not colonized. It was not carved up by foreign powers. It was not burned, invaded, occupied, sanctioned into weakness, and forced to rebuild from national trauma.  China was.  China understands rise and fall. Dynasties. Collapse. Humiliation. Revival. The long rhythm of history.  America understands victory.  So when it finally faces a civilization that grows stronger under pressure, it panics.  Not because China wants to destroy America. But because America knows what it has done to every weaker country it could dominate.  And now it is terrified by one question:  If we lose our monopoly, will China treat us the way we treated the world?

🚨 EXTREMELY ALARMING: POLICE DRONES ARE ALREADY HERE! 🚨 Not in a movie. Not in 2030.  RIGHT NOW in Shenzhen, China... (as well as in America in the attached post) swarms of autonomous police drones hover over city streets like mechanical predators, recording every violation, tracking every car, broadcasting commands from the sky.  You need to see this.   Look at these machines. Red lights blinking in the night sky. Hovering perfectly still above traffic.  They don't just watch... they enforce.  " Your illegal act has been recorded."  Drone "hives" mounted on traffic poles.  24/7 eyes in the sky. Cyberpunk dystopia is live.  This isn't traffic control.   This is the total surveillance state... made flesh.  Shenzhen isn't some backwater experiment...  It's the model city of the future... the same city pushing facial recognition, social credit, digital IDs, and AI governance at warp speed.    While we argue about "privacy rights," China has already deployed the infrastructure for a society where every movement is scored, every mistake is punished, and every citizen is a node in the machine.    Think this stays in China?   Think again.    Western governments and tech giants are salivating over this tech.  DJI drones (yes, the same ones), predictive policing AI, "smart city" contracts... it's all flowing West.  This is exactly why Trump, Kushner, and Elon recently traveled to China.  How are so many people still failing to see what is happening right in front of them?  I have been trying to tell you all this... For nearly a decade now.  London, New York, Paris, Sydney... the pilots are already running.    Your local police department just needs the "right emergency" to roll it out.  Ask yourself the questions they don't want you asking:  - When every street has drone hives, where does "freedom of movement" go?  - When AI flags your "suspicious pattern" before you even act, what happens to free will?  - When your social score drops because a drone saw you jaywalk or stand in the wrong protest, will you still speak?  - Who owns the data?  - Who programs the AI "threat" algorithms?  - What happens when these drones get armed "non-lethally" tasers, sound cannons, net guns?    The technology is here.  The will is the only question left.  This is the Orwellian panopticon... on steroids.  Bentham's prison design where the guard is invisible and omnipresent... except now the guard has perfect memory, flies, and never sleeps.    1984 wasn't a warning.   It was a blueprint.  The most terrifying part...?  Many people want this.  "Less crime!" they say...  "If you've done nothing wrong, you have nothing to hide!" they say...  History's greatest tyrants always sold control as safety.  Elon Musk's grandfather attempted to implement virtually the same plan during the 1930s...  Despite the severity of the inhumane offenses and their devastating consequences, he faced little meaningful accountability, receiving only a $50 fine.  Do you people get it yet?  Every dictatorship started with "Just a few cameras... Just for the criminals... Just temporary."  China shows us the endgame:  - Zero privacy   - Zero dissent   - Total compliance    And the West is importing the hardware while pretending we're still "free."    Digital IDs, CBDCs, carbon tracking, AI content moderation... it's all pieces of the same puzzle.  The drone is just the most visible piece.  Here are some questions that should keep you up at night:  How many freedoms are YOU willing to trade for "safety"?  At what point does "public safety" become state ownership of your life?  If drones can enforce traffic laws today, what will they enforce tomorrow... your speech, your associations, your thoughts?  Who watches the watchers when the watchers are unaccountable machines?  Are you truly a citizen... or just a managed asset in their smart city grid?  This is bigger than one city...  This is the battle for the human soul in the age of AI.    Will we be sovereign individuals?  Or domesticated data points in a global control system?  The window to resist is closing faster than most realize.   What you can do RIGHT NOW:  Share this thread. Make it impossible to ignore.  Demand transparency on drone programs in your city.  Support real privacy legislation that bans persistent aerial surveillance.  Wake up everyone you know... apathy is consent.    The tyrants win when we stay silent.  We are at the fork in the road....  One path: freedom, messy and human.  The other: the machine state where you are never alone and never truly free.    Choose. And choose fast...  The drones are already flying. Read through the responses, engage thoughtfully, ask questions, challenge ideas respectfully, and contribute to meaningful, productive discussion.  And if you're not already following  @TrueOnX... 🚨🇮🇹 Italian confectionery retailer webmail administrator access allegedly listed for sale  A forum actor claims to be selling webmail administrator access and customer data tied to an unidentified Italian online retailer based in Chianciano Terme, Siena. The company reportedly sells traditional Tuscan biscuits, artisan cookies, cantucci, panforte, cakes, pastries, chocolates, and other confectionery products.  The listing includes a screenshot presented as proof of access to the organization’s mailbox, along with a sample of the allegedly obtained customer dataset.  The allegedly exposed information includes customer IDs, full names, tax identification codes, regional and municipal codes, postal addresses, email addresses, telephone and mobile numbers, professions, languages, privacy and marketing consent settings, customer categories, and IP address authorization data.  This claim is currently unverified.  💥 Get early visibility into underground claims, including unblurred screenshots, before they turn into headlines: darkwebinformer.com/pricing

The allegedly exposed information includes full names, RFC and CURP identifiers, employee numbers, departments, positions, employment dates, salaries, payroll concepts, bank account details, birth dates, home addresses, phone numbers, blood types, marital status, education, family information, contract details, tax regimes, and other human resources records. 🚨 The Craneware Group has filed a notice of Cyber Security incident with the London Stock Exchange  londonstockexchange.com/news…  "Craneware (AIM: CRW.L), a leader in healthcare financial performance solutions, reports that it has identified and is responding to a cyber security incident involving unauthorised access to a subset of its data environment.  The Company's incident response plan has been activated, including the appointment by the Board of external cyber security and forensic specialists. Their investigation is ongoing, alongside the Craneware IT team and the Company's retained cyber security service providers.  The incident has been contained and there has been no disruption to customer services or to the Company's operations. The external specialists have confirmed that there are no residual indicators of compromise arising from the cyber security incident in the Company's systems  The Company has notified the relevant regulators and law enforcement agencies, including the Information Commissioner's Office in the UK and Federal Bureau of Investigations in the US.  Investigations so far have established that a significant volume of file names were viewed and exfiltrated. The current assessment is that a large element of the data involved is non-sensitive or already public regulatory data. A percentage of Craneware employee data as well as a subset of customer and partner records have been accessed and exfiltrated.  The Company is continuing to assess the precise nature and scope of all the data involved and is working with its advisers to identify affected parties and prepare appropriate notifications, including any required further notifications to relevant authorities, in each case in accordance with applicable regulatory obligations.  The Company will update the market as appropriate."

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Alleged Just Eat Database Advertised on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a database containing approximately 90 million Just Eat user records on a cybercrime forum.  According to the listing, the alleged dataset includes: * User IDs * Email addresses * Password hashes * Full names * Country information * Currency codes * Account types * Membership dates * Email verification status * Campaign activity * Additional account metadata  The post includes a sample of the purported database but does not provide sufficient evidence to independently verify the authenticity, origin, or recency of the data. Daily Dark Web has not confirmed that the data originated from a compromise of Just Eat or Just Eat Takeaway.com.  Analyst Note: Large consumer database claims are common on cybercrime forums. Many are recycled, aggregated from previous breaches, or artificially inflated. Organizations and users should wait for technical validation before assuming a new compromise has occurred.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #JustEat

🇫🇷 Alleged Stickers-Moto.fr Database Posted on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor has published what they describe as a small SQL database belonging to Stickers-Moto.fr, a French online retailer specializing in motorcycle stickers and accessories.  According to the forum post, the leaked files allegedly include: * User records (Users.csv) * Order history (Commandes.csv) * Session-related data * Customer account information  The post suggests the dataset is relatively small and includes sample records from multiple CSV files. At the time of writing, Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the authenticity of the leaked data or confirmed that it originated from a compromise of Stickers-Moto.fr.  Analyst Note: Even small e-commerce database leaks can expose valuable customer information that enables phishing, credential stuffing, and account takeover attacks, particularly if users have reused passwords across multiple services.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇲🇽 Alleged CECyTE Tabasco Database Leaked  A threat actor has posted what they claim is the full database of CECyTE Tabasco (Colegio de Estudios Científicos y Tecnológicos del Estado de Tabasco), a public educational institution in Mexico.  According to the post, the dataset allegedly includes: * RFC (Mexican Tax ID) * CURP (National ID) * Gmail/email addresses * Usernames * Password hashes * Full names and related account information  The listing references the institution's website (cecytab.edu.mx) and advertises the database as a complete dump. At the time of writing, Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the authenticity of the data or confirmed whether it originated from a compromise of CECyTE Tabasco.  Analyst Note: Educational institutions continue to be attractive targets due to the large volume of student, faculty, and administrative records they maintain. Even when passwords are stored as hashes, exposed identity data such as CURP and RFC can significantly increase the risk of identity theft and phishing.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Mexico

🇮🇩 Alleged Indonesian Government Cyber Division Access Offered for Sale  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is access to an Indonesian government cyber division/police environment on a cybercrime forum.  According to the listing: * Claimed target: Indonesian Government Cyber Division / Police * Access type: Unspecified network or system access * Asking price: $300 * Escrow services accepted * Buyers instructed to contact the seller privately  The listing provides virtually no technical details, proof of access, screenshots, or evidence supporting the claim. As a result, the authenticity, scope, and impact of the alleged access cannot be independently verified.  Analyst Note: Government access advertisements frequently appear on cybercrime forums, but many are fraudulent, outdated, or significantly exaggerated. Organizations should treat such listings as potential early warning indicators while awaiting technical validation or official confirmation.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Indonesia
AI can write code. AI can generate reports. AI can solve complex problems.  But that Excel workbook created in 2013?  That's protected by ancient magic.  #DDW #AI #Excel #TechHumor

🇻🇳 Alleged Database from Vietnamese Website Advertised on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a fresh database from **wasaki.vn**, a Vietnam-based website.  According to the sample shared in the listing, the dataset allegedly includes: * User IDs * Usernames * Password hashes * Email addresses * Display names * Registration timestamps * Account activation keys * User roles * WordPress account metadata  The exposed schema appears consistent with a WordPress user table, suggesting the listing may contain user account information and administrative accounts. However, the authenticity, origin, and recency of the dataset have not been independently verified.  At the time of writing, there is no public confirmation from the organization regarding a security incident matching these claims.  Analyst Note: WordPress-based websites remain frequent targets for attackers exploiting vulnerable plugins, themes, weak credentials, or outdated installations. Even when passwords are hashed, exposed account databases can facilitate credential stuffing, phishing, and further attacks if password reuse is involved.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Vietnam

🇺🇸 Alleged Database from Arizona Caregiver Training Platform Advertised on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a fresh database from **online.arizonacaregivertrain…**, an online caregiver training platform.  According to the sample shared in the listing, the dataset allegedly contains: * User IDs * Usernames * Password hashes * Email addresses * First and last names * Identification numbers * Phone numbers * Institutional and department information * Physical addresses * City and country * Language and timezone settings * Account status and policy information  The post includes a sample of the alleged records and download links. However, the authenticity, origin, and recency of the dataset have not been independently verified.  At the time of writing, there is no public confirmation from the organization regarding a security incident matching these claims.  Analyst Note: Online education and certification platforms often store large amounts of personally identifiable information (PII). If confirmed, a compromise of this nature could expose users to credential stuffing, phishing, and identity theft, particularly if passwords were reused across multiple services.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #USA

🇲🇽 400,327 Mexican Government Supplier Records Allegedly Exposed  A threat actor is claiming that an exposed database belonging to **DIF Tamaulipas** (Sistema para el Desarrollo Integral de la Familia), a family services agency under the Government of Tamaulipas, Mexico, contains information on more than **400,000 supplier registrations**.  According to the post, the exposed records allegedly include: * Supplier names (individuals and companies) * Mexican Tax IDs (RFC) * Full physical addresses * Municipalities and states * Legal representative names * Business categories * Contact phone numbers * Email addresses * Internal registration numbers  The actor claims the database was accessible without authentication and provides a description of the exposed fields. However, the authenticity of the data, the exposure method, and whether the database remains publicly accessible have not been independently verified.  At the time of writing, there has been no public confirmation from DIF Tamaulipas regarding the alleged exposure.  Analyst Note: Government supplier databases often contain sensitive business and personally identifiable information. If confirmed, exposures of this nature can increase the risk of procurement fraud, business email compromise (BEC), identity theft, and targeted phishing campaigns against registered vendors.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Mexico

🇮🇩 Alleged Indonesian National Vaccine Database Advertised on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a second database from Indonesia's national vaccine system.  According to the listing, the dataset allegedly contains: * National Identification Numbers (NIK) * Full names * Places and dates of birth * Residential addresses * Gender * Telephone numbers * District and subdistrict information * Administrative records * Creation and update timestamps  The forum post describes the data as a "second database" and includes a sample of the alleged records. However, the authenticity, origin, and scope of the dataset have not been independently verified.  At the time of writing, there is no public confirmation from Indonesian authorities regarding a breach matching these claims.  Analyst Note: Government health databases remain attractive targets due to the volume of personally identifiable information they contain. However, threat actors frequently repackage or relist previously leaked datasets, making independent validation essential before drawing conclusions.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Indonesia

🇬🇹 Alleged Guatemalan Citizen Database Advertised with Election-Related Claims  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a database containing personal information on Guatemalan citizens, while also alleging, without providing evidence, that the data was used to manipulate Guatemala's 2023 elections.  According to the listing, the dataset allegedly contains: * National identification numbers * Full names * Dates of birth * Tax identification numbers * Email addresses * Profile photographs (for some individuals)  The post includes a sample of the alleged data and download links. However, the authenticity of the dataset and the accompanying claims regarding election manipulation have not been independently verified.  At the time of writing, there is no publicly available evidence confirming that this dataset was involved in Guatemala's 2023 election process or that it originated from an election-related system.  Analyst Note: Threat actors frequently attach sensational political narratives to leaked datasets to increase visibility and perceived value. The existence of a dataset does not substantiate broader claims of election fraud or interference, which require independent forensic and investigative evidence.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Guatemala

🇧🇷 Alleged 20,000-Record Database from Brazilian E-Commerce Site Advertised on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a fresh database from **lojanegociosdigital.com.br**, a Brazilian e-commerce platform, containing approximately **20,000 customer records**.  According to the listing and sample, the dataset allegedly includes: * Customer IDs * Billing email addresses * Order and transaction IDs * Payment method details * Order status and timestamps * Billing and IP addresses * Currency and tax information * WooCommerce order metadata  The forum post labels the dataset as a recent leak and requires forum credits to access the download. However, the authenticity, origin, and freshness of the data have not been independently verified.  At the time of writing, there is no public confirmation from the organization regarding a security incident matching these claims.  Analyst Note: Smaller e-commerce platforms running WooCommerce remain frequent targets for attackers exploiting vulnerable plugins, weak administrative credentials, or outdated software. Until independently validated, the claimed compromise should be treated as unverified.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Brazil

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-21 08:01 KST

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 위협 환경은 AI 기술을 활용한 공격 자동화와 공급망 공격이 결합된 형태로 진화하고 있습니다. 특히 GitHub 저장소를 악용한 SmartLoader 유포 및 AI 모델의 scaffolding을 활용한 자율적 공격 연결이 확인되고 있으며, 이는 기존의 정적 방어 체계를 무력화하고 있습니다([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/fakegit-campaign-uses-7600-github.html), [Malwarebytes Labs](https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/threat-intel/2026/07/fake-games-spread-stealers-with-renpy-loader-msbuild-and-etherhiding)). 또한, SonicWall 및 WordPress 취약점(CVE-2026-63030)과 같이 패치 전 제로데이(Zero-day)를 악용한 커스텀 악성코드 배포가 활발하며, 기업의 AI 도입 가속화에 따른 보안 거버넌스 부재가 주요 리스크로 부상했습니다([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/cyberattacks-data-breaches/wp2shell-millions-wordpress-sites-remote-takeover), [BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/sonicwall-sma1000-flaws-exploited-as-zero-days-to-push-custom-malware/)).

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **UTA0533**: SonicWall SMA1000 제로데이(CVE-2026-15409, CVE-2026-15410)를 악용하여 커스텀 악성코드를 배포([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/sonicwall-zero-days-exploited-to-deliver-custom-malware-for-weeks-before-patch/)).
- **Lazarus Group**: MetaMask 개발자로 위장한 인력을 통해 암호화폐 플랫폼 침투 및 코드 조작 시도([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/hackers-steal-237-million-in-crypto-from-ostium-in-off-chain-attack/)).
- **Laundry Bear**: NATO/EU Microsoft 365 환경을 대상으로 Evilginx AitM 및 QR 피싱을 통해 데이터 탈취([DFIR_Radar](https://twitter.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2078873691950076321)).
- **ShinyHunters / ShadowByt3$**: Abbott Labs 대상 데이터 탈취 및 갈취 시도([Malwarebytes Labs](https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/data-breaches/2026/07/healthcare-giant-abbott-probes-two-cyber-incidents-amid-extortion-claims)).

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **해양수산부**: AI 응용제품 신속상용화 지원사업 착수(보안 강화 필요성 증대) ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207699)).
- **이스트시큐리티(ESRC)**: 세무 위반 통지 사칭 피싱 메일 및 북한발 npm 악성 패키지(chai-as-init) 유포 주의보 발령 ([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5771), [이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5766)).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-63030**: WordPress 원격 코드 실행 취약점, 현재 대규모 악용 중 ([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/cyberattacks-data-breaches/wp2shell-millions-wordpress-sites-remote-takeover)).
- **CVE-2026-60137**: WordPress 취약점, CVE-2026-63030과 연계되어 공격에 활용 ([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/cyberattacks-data-breaches/wp2shell-millions-wordpress-sites-remote-takeover)).
- **CVE-2026-15409 / CVE-2026-15410**: SonicWall SMA1000 제로데이, 커스텀 악성코드 배포에 악용 ([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/sonicwall-zero-days-exploited-to-deliver-custom-malware-for-weeks-before-patch/)).

### 5. IOC 요약
- **CVE**: CVE-2026-63030 ([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/cyberattacks-data-breaches/wp2shell-millions-wordpress-sites-remote-takeover)), CVE-2026-60137 ([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/cyberattacks-data-breaches/wp2shell-millions-wordpress-sites-remote-takeover)), CVE-2026-15409 ([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/sonicwall-zero-days-exploited-to-deliver-custom-malware-for-weeks-before-patch/)), CVE-2026-15410 ([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/sonicwall-zero-days-exploited-to-deliver-custom-malware-for-weeks-before-patch/)).

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **WordPress 패치**: CVE-2026-63030 및 CVE-2026-60137 관련 최신 보안 패치를 즉시 적용하고 웹 서버 로그에서 비정상적인 요청 패턴을 모니터링하십시오.
- **SonicWall 장비 점검**: SMA1000 시리즈 장비의 펌웨어 업데이트를 확인하고, 외부 노출된 관리자 페이지에 대한 접근 제어(ACL)를 강화하십시오.
- **공급망 보안**: npm 등 오픈소스 패키지 사용 시 `chai-as-init`와 같은 의심스러운 패키지 포함 여부를 검사하고, 개발 환경에서의 자동화된 코드 실행을 제한하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가

news_articles.title: 해양수산부, 해양수산 AI 응용제품 신속상용화 지원사업 본격 착수
